reviews.author: aya halitim
reviews.text: What a magical place. The ticket includes 3 waterfalls. Rhe village is literally a village..it's all connected. Be prepared to walk a lot and be prepared to get dirty . Gorgeous place. Get yourself a good camera or phone before starting your trip here. Every corner is a beautiful place to take pics Be prepared to bargain with the motorbike drivers too because you need a motorbike to pick you up to the village. The only inconvenience is the dirty old stairs. Too difficult to walk through

reviews.author: libby
reviews.text: Our experience was okay. Nice to see an old village! Definitely aimed more for people who like taking thousands of pictures! Waterfall is a nice feature! Full of shops selling the same things over and over again. Definitely a tourist trap! Slide is an extra 40,000 VND. Ride back up to the Exit is 10,000 VND each. Enter Cat Cat is 150,000 VND per adult. We wouldn’t go again or recommend it to people.

reviews.author: Arvs
reviews.text: If you will go to Cat Cat, we suggest to rent the traditional clothes inside, since from the tickets to the place it self, it is quite a distance and so that you will not be that stressed as well. Great place for picture taking and also different and lots of place to eat and chill. We suggest as well to eat the barbeque and sausages there when you get hungry.

reviews.author: Manteli Rates
reviews.text: I walked here and it wasn’t that bad. The village itself costs 150 k. This place was made for Instagram photos and influencers but I still enjoyed the visit. Views were nice.

reviews.author: Pooriya
reviews.text: It is a nice environment and worth a visit. At first, you may feel disappointed. Towards the end, you see traditional dancing and small shops. The views are good. I would recommend visiting, but you do not miss much if you skip it.

reviews.author: Prakash Garg
reviews.text: The weather was little foggy. That’s why I couldn’t enjoy the view at its best, but the place is extremely beautiful, specially there auditorium. I am impressed with the fact that most of it is sustainable structure with a little bit of artificial plantation, but what they have created is very beautiful. You should check the time and enjoy their cultural performance inside the cat cat village. The shops are quite good. You will get a lot of souvenirs for your friends and family. A very good place for the photo shoot. They have the ethnic wear available on rent which you can wear and get the traditional photo shoot done. The place was on walking distance from our hotel, and we spend almost full day here

reviews.author: Elyana Kusuma
reviews.text: August is a highly coveted month for experiencing the breathtaking beauty of Sapa and Cat Cat Village, a charming mountainous area nestled in the northern region of Vietnam. This picturesque paradise boasts of its mesmerizing terraced rice fields, enchanting valley vistas, and rich cultural diversity of its ethnic groups. During August, Sapa flaunts its verdant rice terraces in all its glory, showcasing a luscious shade of green just before the harvest season commences. Despite occasional rainfall, the misty weather adds a mystical touch to the already captivating mountain landscape. Moreover, the drizzle brings respite from the scorching heat, making it the perfect weather for indulging in invigorating hikes.

reviews.author: Jonathan Phan
reviews.text: Visiting Cat Cat viewpoint in Sa Pa during the third week of June 2024 with my family was an unforgettable experience. The weather was perfect, with just enough sunshine peeking through the clouds to illuminate the verdant rice terraces that Sa Pa is renowned for. The bamboo structure at the viewpoint provided a unique frame to view and capture photos of the stunning mountainous landscape. It’s a serene spot that invites reflection and appreciation for nature’s beauty. As my journey in Sa Pa came to a close, I found myself at this very spot soaking in every last detail—the cool breeze and tranquility. It was more than just a visual treat; it was a moment that engaged all senses and left me with a profound sense of peace.

reviews.author: Veronika Hubertová
reviews.text: Tourist trap, if you don't have much time in Sapa, skip this place.

reviews.author: Engr Myo Myint Tun
reviews.text: This place is not Cat Cat Village. Better don’t go. Very far from Sapa city, road is narrow and steep. Entrance fee is 150,000 VND.

reviews.author: faizal bakhtiar
reviews.text: Instagrammable view point. It gives an aerial view of the village. The 3 storey structure was built for taking pictures. It was very popular with tourists

reviews.author: Alon
reviews.text: Highly touristic sight with fake waterfalls you can literally see that they diverted water and pipes to make waterfalls. Other than that I consider to be very inauthentic

reviews.author: TravelbyTin
reviews.text: Very bad the altitude of the staffs. Tourist trap!!!

reviews.author: J J Haris
reviews.text: This is an essential tour in Sa Pa. Beautiful landscape of the valley, padi terraces and vegetable plantation. You can rent a traditional costume and tour cat cat village. It provide picturesque background for your photos. Beautiful.

reviews.author: Paul S
reviews.text: MOTORCYCLE SCAM (transport from Cat Cat village ticket entrance to Cat Cat Village) - Take village electric cart for 10,000 per person per trip (but may need to wait long time to fill up a cart) Walk to village within 15 mins Motorcycle drivers ask for 100k round trip. Fast and efficient but must bargain hard if you do this

reviews.author: Gregg Chiong
reviews.text: Cat Cat Viewpoint is so amazing. Must visit if you plan to travel to Sapa Vietnam

reviews.author: allan khoo
reviews.text: Cat Cat Village offers a glimpse into the unique culture and natural beauty of the Sapa region, making it a must-visit destination for travelers to Vietnam.

reviews.author: Indira Defa Fortuna
reviews.text: Very cute place, some of the sellers meow for no reason (because cat cat), the actual cats inside however is leashed - to avoid running into the higher dog population, I suppose? Tip from a fellow Southeast Asian: Use bamboo. The wood used as stairs and platforming at the photo spot is getting weak because of moisture damage. Scary and slippery.

reviews.author: Xiaolin Laoshi
reviews.text: Amazing landscaping view and good place for the people who love to touch the nature

reviews.author: Diana Shakirova
reviews.text: Entrance is very expensive Once you get inside it’s only shops and people are there so demanding Won’t go there even for free if I knew it

reviews.author: Beatriz Goulart Pinheiro
reviews.text: The place may be beautiful, but it's a tourist trap. We thought we were paying in order to help preserve the cultural heritage, but inside it's a complete scam full of people selling trinkets. Not worthy.

reviews.author: Ric
reviews.text: Tourist trap, 150k entrance fee to see the classic tourist traps

reviews.author: Daina Asri Pradhita (Dhita)
reviews.text: the place is very clean with a beautiful landscape but it didn’t quite live up to my expectations in terms of cultural immersion

reviews.author: Pragati Manerkar
reviews.text: The overall SAPA experience was good. The view points are beautiful.

reviews.author: Simon Setiawan
reviews.text: recommended to rent a local dress and photographer

reviews.author: ASM Playground
reviews.text: Just casual, expensive ticket nothing you can found
